title: Jeju Island 2-Day Nature Retreat
metaTitle: Jeju Island 2-Day Nature Retreat | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Jeju Island, South Korea: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak) and Manjanggul Lava Tube. Prices from $1335 per person (estimate).
highlights: Hiking Hallasan to the summit crater lake
Sunrise hike up Seongsan Ilchulbong together
Udo Island (bike loop and peanut ice cream)
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Jeju Island, South Korea — Temperate maritime climate — mild winters (rarely below 5°C), warm humid summers (June-August, highs around 29-31°C with a rainy season and occasional typhoons), and comfortable spring/autumn shoulder seasons famous for canola-flower and cherry-blossom blooms. May and September to October offer mild temperatures, blooming or fresh scenery, and lighter crowds than the peak Korean summer holiday season. This itinerary blends Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak), Manjanggul Lava Tube, Hallasan National Park trails with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak), Manjanggul Lava Tube, Hallasan National Park trails
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Jeju Island
itinerary.description: Land at Jeju International Airport (CJU) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak) — UNESCO-listed volcanic tuff cone with sweeping sunrise views. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Jeju Island Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Manjanggul Lava Tube — One of the world's finest lava tube cave systems, walkable for 1km. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to CJU for your onward flight.

travelTips: Rent a car if possible — Jeju's best sights are spread across the island and public transit is limited outside the main towns.
Book Hallasan summit trail permits/timed slots in advance during peak season.
Try Jeju's famous black pork (heukdwaeji) — it's a genuinely distinct local specialty, not just a marketing label.
Seongsan Ilchulbong is best visited at sunrise both for the light and to avoid tour-bus crowds.
Download the Kakao Map app — Google Maps has limited functionality for driving directions in South Korea.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Hiking shoes for Hallasan and Olle Trail sections
Windbreaker — coastal winds are strong year-round
Rain gear for the June-July wet season
thingsToDo: Hiking Hallasan to the summit crater lake
Scuba diving with Jeju's haenyeo dive sites
thingsToAvoid: Relying solely on Google Maps for driving directions — use Kakao Map or Naver Map instead.
Treating haenyeo (traditional divers) demonstrations as a casual photo-op without respect for the tradition.
Visiting Seongsan Ilchulbong or the canola flower fields at midday in peak season, when tour buses crowd every viewpoint.
Underestimating Hallasan's summit hike — it's a genuine full-day, ~1,950m ascent requiring an early start.
topRestaurants: Jeju black pork BBQ houses (Heukdwaeji Geori) — Korean BBQ ($$)
Myeongjin Jeonbok (abalone rice porridge) — Korean Seafood ($$)
Halmang Jip — Traditional Jeju Home Cooking ($$)
Dombegogi restaurants near Jungmun — Jeju Pork Specialty ($$)
Olle Market food stalls — Korean Street Food ($)
Seongsan seafood raw fish (hoe) restaurants — Fresh Raw Seafood ($$$)
nightlife: Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market evening stalls
hiddenGems: Udo Island bike loop (a short ferry from Seongsan)
adventureActivities: Hiking Hallasan to the summit crater lake
Scuba diving with Jeju's haenyeo dive sites
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at a Jungmun resort with ocean views
Private abalone and black pork tasting course
Helicopter tour over Hallasan and the coastline
Spa day at a resort onsen-style bathhouse
honeymoonExperiences: Sunrise hike up Seongsan Ilchulbong together
Private couples' photo shoot amid canola flower fields
Udo Island day trip by bike and ferry
budgetExperiences: Olle Trail sections cost nothing beyond good shoes
Free coastal viewpoints at Yongduam Rock and Iho Beach
Hallasan National Park hiking (entry is free)
localEtiquette: Bowing is a common greeting alongside handshakes; a slight bow when greeting elders or service staff is appreciated.
Remove shoes before entering traditional guesthouses (hanok) and many homes.
Two hands (or supporting your forearm) should be used when giving or receiving items, especially with elders.
Haenyeo (Jeju's famous free-diving "sea women") are a source of local pride — treat their diving demonstrations respectfully, not as a photo prop.
Quiet, orderly queuing is the norm, including at popular photo spots.
localTransportGuide: Renting a car is by far the most convenient way to explore Jeju's spread-out attractions, and an international driving permit is required. Public buses cover the main towns and attractions but run less frequently in rural areas. Taxis and ride-hailing (Kakao T) are reliable for shorter hops within Jeju City or Seogwipo.
bestTimeToVisit: May and September to October offer mild temperatures, blooming or fresh scenery, and lighter crowds than the peak Korean summer holiday season.
weather: Temperate maritime climate — mild winters (rarely below 5°C), warm humid summers (June-August, highs around 29-31°C with a rainy season and occasional typhoons), and comfortable spring/autumn shoulder seasons famous for canola-flower and cherry-blossom blooms.
visaInformation: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and EU citizens can enter South Korea visa-free for 30-90 days for tourism (K-ETA pre-authorization required for many nationalities). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~10.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Jeju Island?
faqs.answer: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia, and EU citizens can enter South Korea visa-free for 30-90 days for tourism (K-ETA pre-authorization required for many nationalities). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Jeju Island?
faqs.answer: May and September to October offer mild temperatures, blooming or fresh scenery, and lighter crowds than the peak Korean summer holiday season. Temperate maritime climate — mild winters (rarely below 5°C), warm humid summers (June-August, highs around 29-31°C with a rainy season and occasional typhoons), and comfortable spring/autumn shoulder seasons famous for canola-flower and cherry-blossom blooms.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Jeju Island safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Jeju Island is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Jeju Island?
faqs.answer: The local currency is KRW. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Jeju Island is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunrise hike up Seongsan Ilchulbong together experience. It also works well for families — highlights include Teddy Bear Museum.

reviews.author: Sofia Patel
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Jeju Loveland in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Olivia Fernandez
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Jeju Island was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Manjanggul Lava Tube was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Botha
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Hallasan National Park trails and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Anna Fernandez
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Jeju Island is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Yongduam Rock (Dragon Head Rock). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Arjun Cohen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Teddy Bear Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Daniel Johnson
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak) and Yongduam Rock (Dragon Head Rock). Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Noah Garcia
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Jeju Island was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Iho Tewoo Beach was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Sarah Smith
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Jeju Island is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Hallasan National Park trails. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Noah Rossi

reviews.body: Our trip to Jeju Island was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Spirited Garden (Bonsai Garden) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
